releases.title: collectZ v3.7.3
releases.summary: This patch continues the human-readable timeline work by giving deleted or archived activity rows a readable snapshot drawer.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added an Activity snapshot drawer for timeline rows whose original target is no longer openable.
Added `View snapshot` actions for deleted or archived timeline entries that still have saved audit details.
Shows the saved title, target, action, timestamp, user, type, provider, scope hints, and reason when those fields exist.
Keeps the raw activity payload behind a technical disclosure.
Added browser coverage for a deleted media row opening its saved timeline snapshot.

releases.title: collectZ v3.7.2
releases.summary: This patch makes sync failures and sync activity inspectable from the Dashboard and Activity timeline instead of sending users to broad import or integration screens.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared sync-job detail drawer backed by `/api/media/sync-jobs/:id/result`.
Changed Dashboard failed-sync rows to open detailed job readback with status, provider, timestamps, failure text, summary counters, progress, and technical payload.
Added timeline actions for sync jobs and import activity so failed rows can open `Open failure` and successful rows can open `Open sync`.
Kept existing import navigation available for import-shaped activity rows.
Added source-level regression coverage to keep the Dashboard, Activity timeline, and sync-job drawer wired together.

releases.title: collectZ v3.7.1
releases.summary: This patch keeps iterating on the human-readable activity timeline by adding contextual navigation from timeline rows to the related app surface.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added small timeline action buttons for known targets such as media items, Art, Collectibles, Events, imports, loans, integrations, people, workspaces, and users.
Wired Admin Activity links into the main dashboard shell so platform timeline entries can open the relevant admin or library surface.
Wired Workspace Activity links so workspace entries can open the matching workspace tab or jump out to the relevant library item.
Added focus handoff for media, Art, Collectible, and Event targets so existing rows can open their detail drawer after navigation.
Extended the workspace activity browser regression to prove a media timeline row can open the related library detail.

releases.title: collectZ v3.7.0
releases.summary: This minor milestone turns the existing technical activity feed into a readable timeline for people using the app day to day, while keeping the raw audit details available when operators need them.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Promoted `Human-Readable Activity Timeline` from the product backlog into the `3.7.0` roadmap.
Reworked the shared Activity surface so Admin Activity and Workspace Activity show readable timeline entries first.

Added timeline copy for imports, scanner imports, Plex and Kavita provider activity, ratings, watched-state changes, valuation refreshes, library changes, wishlist conversions, workspace people/invite activity, security events, and failures.
Added filters for imports, providers, library, events, people, security, and failures.
Moved raw action names, exact timestamps, IPs, statuses, reasons, and JSON details behind a `Technical details` disclosure.
Moved the working app/version metadata to `3.7.0` so the minor milestone is aligned across app meta, packages, public compose defaults, release notes, and the in-app release feed.

releases.title: collectZ v3.6.0
releases.summary: This minor milestone starts first-class wishlist and acquisition tracking: a way to track things users want without pretending they already own them.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Promoted `Acquisition, Wishlist, and Want Tracking` from the product backlog into the `3.6.0` roadmap.
Added scoped `wanted_items` storage with `wanted`, `watching`, `preordered`, `ordered`, `acquired`, and `dismissed` states.
Added authenticated `/api/wishlist` endpoints to list, create, edit, delete, and convert media-compatible wishlist items into canonical library media rows.
Added a Library > Wishlist surface with status tabs, type/search filters, lightweight acquisition metadata, and an `Add to library` conversion action.
Documented the wishlist API in OpenAPI and added browser regression coverage for listing and converting a wanted item.
Moved the working app/version metadata to `3.6.0` so the minor milestone is aligned across app meta, packages, public compose defaults, release notes, and the in-app release feed.

releases.title: collectZ v3.5.1
releases.summary: This patch tightens the new Dashboard command center so attention items show useful readback instead of sending users to broad navigation destinations.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Removed the superfluous Dashboard `Quick actions` panel.
Removed the `Import` shortcut from `Recent syncs`.
Removed the duplicate `Latest failures` panel so failed syncs have one source of truth in `Needs attention`.
Changed failed sync attention from a generic Integrations jump into inline failed-job details with provider, job type, timestamp, status, and error readback.
Added inline lists for items missing covers and items missing identifiers.
Made the summary counters and attention tab actions open filtered all-media Library review lists for missing covers and missing identifiers.
Added `review_filter` support to `GET /api/media` so Dashboard drilldowns and Library use the same backend source of truth for attention lists.
Made Dashboard more compact with tighter panel spacing, smaller summary counters, and capped attention samples.
Removed the aggregate `Needs attention` summary metric because it duplicated the tabbed attention panel without adding a distinct workflow.
Reflowed Dashboard into a denser dashboard layout with three collection-health metrics, a primary attention/provider row, and compact Recent syncs, Recent activity, and Upcoming events panels.
Renamed the command-center surface to `Dashboard` across the nav, heading, tab id, API route, OpenAPI contract, tests, and docs.
Changed `Needs attention` into tabs so failed syncs, missing covers, missing identifiers, and Plex conflicts do not stack into a long page on high-resolution screens.
Extended `GET /api/dashboard/summary` with `attention_details` samples for missing-cover and missing-identifier items.
Updated OpenAPI and source assertions for the richer Dashboard summary contract.

releases.title: collectZ v3.5.0
releases.summary: This minor release starts the `3.5` line with a new Dashboard command center: a first-screen operational summary of what changed, what failed, and what needs attention in the active workspace/library scope.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added authenticated `GET /api/dashboard/summary` for scoped Dashboard data.
Dashboard now summarizes collection totals, missing covers, missing identifiers, recent syncs, failed syncs, open Plex reconciliation conflicts, provider status, upcoming Events, and recent activity.
`/dashboard` now opens Dashboard by default for normal platform and homelab users.
Added Dashboard navigation and quick links back into Import, Library, Events, and Integrations.
Documented the Dashboard summary endpoint in OpenAPI and mapped PAT access to `media:read`.
Promoted `Dashboard / Command Center` out of the backlog and into the `3.5.0` roadmap.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.162
releases.summary: This patch keeps valid ISBN scanner and web lookups on the Books/ISBN path even when the Books provider has no match.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Valid ISBN-10/ISBN-13 barcode values now return `provider: books:isbn-direct` from scanner lookup without falling through to UPCItemDB/general barcode lookup.
ISBN no-match lookups return a successful empty `matches` list instead of a potentially unrelated barcode-provider candidate.
Checksum-valid non-ISBN EAN/UPC values, such as `0076783005990`, remain on the barcode-provider path so provider title variants still surface.
The web barcode lookup path follows the same ISBN no-fallback behavior.
Public templates, browser metadata, package names, generated compose defaults, and operator docs now use `collectZ`/`collectz` naming instead of the old app-name defaults.
Scanner smoke coverage now proves ISBN direct success and ISBN direct no-match behavior for both scanner and web lookup routes.
OpenAPI now documents that ISBN no-match scans stay on `books:isbn-direct`.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.161
releases.summary: This patch makes scanner barcode imports run the existing backend enrichment pipeline before creating or updating the canonical media row.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: `POST /api/media/import-barcode` now enriches the selected scanner match with available ISBN, UPC, title, and provider data before upsert.
ISBN book imports can populate Books/Google Books metadata such as overview, cover image, publisher, and type details.
Import responses now include `enrichment_status`, `lookup_path`, and `lookup_status` so scanner clients can show what happened.
Barcode import activity and metadata record the scanner enrichment outcome for readback/debugging.
Scanner smoke now proves ISBN import enrichment end-to-end with a fake Books provider.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.160
releases.summary: This patch makes ISBN-shaped barcode scans use the configured Books provider directly, so the iOS scanner app can get Google Books-style candidates without routing through the web UI or forcing the client to know the media type first.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Scanner barcode lookup now detects ISBN-10/ISBN-13 shaped codes automatically.
Web UPC lookup now also uses the direct ISBN path when a scanned identifier normalizes to ISBN.
Direct ISBN matches return with `source: books:isbn-direct` and `mediaTypeGuess: book`.
Scanner smoke now proves the ISBN path works without `mediaType: book`.
OpenAPI now documents scanner ISBN behavior on `/api/media/lookup/barcode`.
